title: Cleaner Air Spaces Act of 2025
plain_summary: The Environmental Protection Agency will give money to local air quality agencies to help protect people from wildfire smoke. These agencies must work with community groups to set up special clean air rooms in public buildings where people can go when the air outside is smoky and dangerous. They also have to give out free air cleaning machines to families who do not have much money and are at risk from breathing wildfire smoke. The program must include at least one tribal agency and will help teach people about staying safe during wildfires.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Environment and Public Works.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Cleaner Air Spaces Act of twenty twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2  Cleaner Air Space Program Grants
sections.text:
In this section, several terms have specific meanings. The Administrator means the person who runs the Environmental Protection Agency. An air pollution control agency is defined by an existing law called the Clean Air Act. A clean air center means one or more clean air rooms in a building that the public can visit. A clean air room is a room designed to keep harmful air pollution as low as possible when wildfire smoke is in the area.

A covered household is a family that lives in a low income community and includes someone who is likely to experience wildfire smoke events and could be hurt by that smoke because of health problems, disabilities, or age. An eligible air filtration unit is an air cleaner that meets several requirements. It must be certified to remove at least ninety seven units of smoke particles per minute. It must be certified under the Energy Star program. It cannot produce ozone gas. It must use a special filter that removes ninety nine point nine seven percent of tiny particles.

A low income community has the same meaning as defined in tax law. The Administrator must give grants to air pollution control agencies to run cleaner air space programs if Congress provides the money.

The Administrator cannot give more than three million dollars to any single air pollution control agency. The Administrator must give at least one grant to a Tribal agency that controls air quality.

To apply for a grant, an air pollution control agency must send an application to the Administrator. The application must include a plan for running a cleaner air space program. The plan must show that the agency will work with a community based organization. It must explain what each group will do in the program. It must identify which families will receive free air cleaners. It must explain how the agency will share educational materials about air cleaners and advertise clean air centers. It must describe where the clean air center will be located and how big it will be. It must list all the costs of running the program.

If an air pollution control agency gets a grant and works with a community organization as required, it must do several things. It must create at least one clean air center in an area that might have wildfire smoke. The center must be easy for covered households to reach. The center must be open and have staff during wildfire smoke events and may be open at other times too.

The agency must tell the public about the clean air center when there is wildfire smoke. It must advertise the whole program including information about free air cleaners, who can get them, and who to contact for more information. The agency must give away at least one thousand eligible air filtration units to covered households for free. It must also give one replacement filter for each air cleaner.

The agency must hand out educational materials that teach people how to use air cleaners to make clean air rooms in their homes. The agency must keep track of what types of air cleaners it gives out, how many it distributes, and how much each type costs. It must share this information with the Administrator.

Within six months of giving an air cleaner to a covered household, the agency must survey someone from that household without asking for their name. The survey must ask if they understood how to set up a clean air room and use the air cleaner. It must ask how often they used the air cleaner and what made it hard to use properly. It must ask if the air seemed cleaner in their clean air room compared to other parts of their home. It must ask how the program could be better.

Any air pollution control agency that gets a grant must work with at least one community based organization to carry out all these requirements.

No later than three years after this law is passed, the Administrator must send a report to Congress. The report must include information about each cleaner air space program that used grant money. This includes the name of each agency that got a grant and the information they collected about air cleaners they gave out. The report must include answers from the surveys. The report must make recommendations about whether the grant program should be expanded and how it can be improved.

Congress authorizes thirty million dollars to be spent on this program from fiscal year twenty twenty six through fiscal year twenty twenty eight. The Administrator can use up to ten percent of this money to pay for running the grant program.
